name: Río Tea and Alto do Marco Loop
name_original: A Freixa a Salgueirón — circular por las Playas fluviales de río Tea y el Alto de Marco
description: Hike the difficult 11.8-mile Río Tea and Alto do Marco Loop, gaining 1999 feet through diverse forests and historical river landscapes.
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails in Zec Río Tea traverse a region characterized by the Tea River, which carves a diverse course through riverine forests and valleys. This Natura 2000 Special Area of Conservation features a rich ecological setting with species like willow, alder, and oak. The terrain varies from mountainous sections to open valleys, offering a blend of natural beauty and historical points of interest. Best family friendly hiking trails in Zec Río Tea The most popular family friendly hiking route is As Amolares Footbridge – Parque das Tres Pontes loop from Mondariz Balneario, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 9 minutes to complete, offering an easy walk through riverside scenery. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cernadela Bridge – Gran Hotel de Mondariz loop from Mondariz Balneario, an easy 4.0 miles (6.5 km) path. This route features the historic Cernadela Bridge and the grandeur of Mondariz-Balneario's architecture. Local hikers also love the Fonte de Gándara – Cernadela Bridge loop from Mondariz Balneario, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes and past the Roman-origin Cernadela Bridge, often completed in about 2 hours 4 minutes. Hiking in Zec Río Tea is defined by its extensive riverine forests, the varied course of the Tea River, and historical elements like the Cernadela Bridge and ancient "poldras." The network offers options for easy family friendly hiking trails as well as more moderate routes. The routes in Zec Río Tea are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 200 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Zec Río Tea's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are available in Zec Río Tea?
A: Zec Río Tea offers a wide selection of routes suitable for families. You'll find over 50 hiking trails, with more than 20 of them classified as easy, making them perfect for a relaxed family outing.

Q: What makes Zec Río Tea a good destination for family hikes?
A: The region is a Natura 2000 Special Area of Conservation, boasting diverse riverine landscapes and rich biodiversity. The trails often follow the Tea River, offering gentle paths through beautiful riverine forests of willow, alder, and oak, which are ideal for families to explore nature together. The clean waters and varied vegetation also provide habitats for interesting wildlife.

Q: Are there any easy, short loop trails suitable for families with young children?
A: Yes, there are several excellent short loop trails. For a very accessible option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31896967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Amolares Footbridge – Parque das Tres Pontes loop from Mondariz Balneario&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 4.4 km route. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14934056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cernadela Bridge – Gran Hotel de Mondariz loop from Mondariz Balneario&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6.5 km trail that passes by historical sites.

Q: Can we bring our dog on the family-friendly trails in Zec Río Tea?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in Zec Río Tea. However, as it is a Natura 2000 Special Area of Conservation, it's important to keep your dog on a leash to protect the local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all visitors. Always remember to clean up after your pet.

Q: What kind of natural scenery can we expect to see on these family hikes?
A: You can expect to immerse yourselves in lush riverine forests, with the Tea River as a constant companion. The river's course offers dynamic changes in scenery, from mountainous sections to open valleys. Keep an eye out for 'rocky wonders' and the diverse plant life that thrives along the riverbanks, creating a vibrant ecological setting.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to discover along the trails?
A: Absolutely! Many trails incorporate fascinating historical elements. You can visit the Roman-origin &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1283130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge of the Partitions&lt;/a&gt;, the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1262296" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Remedios Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1171542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabalón Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features ancient 'Poldras' or 'Pasos' – stone crossings in the riverbed – and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4718152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gran Hotel de Mondariz&lt;/a&gt; adds a touch of grandeur.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking with family in Zec Río Tea?
A: The region is beautiful year-round, but spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant conditions for family hikes. The weather is generally milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant with spring blooms or rich autumn colors. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially along the shaded river trails, but be mindful of warmer temperatures.

Q: Are there trails that offer views of waterfalls or interesting river features?
A: Yes, the Tea River is known for its eye-catching cascades and serene streams. While specific large waterfalls might not be highlighted on every family trail, the river itself provides numerous smaller cascades and the unique 'Dos Pasos Ford of Tatín,' which offers easy access to the river and is a beautiful spot to explore.

Q: Where can we find parking for the family-friendly trailheads?
A: Many of the family-friendly routes, especially those starting near Mondariz Balneario, have accessible parking options. For example, trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10457500" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fonte de Gándara – Cernadela Bridge loop&lt;/a&gt; often have parking available in or near the town, making it convenient for families.

Q: What do other hikers say about the family-friendly trails in Zec Río Tea?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars. Reviewers often praise the tranquil river paths, the well-maintained trails, and the beautiful natural surroundings that make for a relaxing and enjoyable experience for all ages.

Q: Are there any places to eat or get refreshments near the family-friendly routes?
A: Mondariz-Balneario, a small but charming municipality, is often a starting point or passes through many trails. Here you can find cafes and restaurants to refuel after your hike. It's a great place to enjoy a meal or a refreshing drink with your family.

Q: Are there any specific trails that are particularly good for spotting wildlife?
A: The entire Zec Río Tea is rich in biodiversity. The riverine forests and clean waters create ideal habitats for a wide array of invertebrates, amphibians, mammals, and birds. While hiking along routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375297838" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Tea River Trail&lt;/a&gt;, keep an eye out for species like the Pyrenean desman, endemic long-tailed salamander, and various bird species that frequent the riverbanks.
tips: The constant flow of the Tea River southwards into the Miño River creates these natural pools on the banks of the river for everyone to enjoy. Jump in to cool off on a summer day.

The walk to Salgueirón from the Tea River valley will be tough, but rewarding. The path runs through a mixed terrain of forest trails surrounded by pine trees and mature oak trees. The panoramic views from the top are spectacular on a busy day.
A beautiful river beach on the Tea River north of the town of Ponteareas. Enjoy a wild swim in this unique river landscape.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Río Tea and Alto do Marco Loop?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult. While sections along the Río Tea can be moderate, the inclusion of the Alto do Marco significantly increases the challenge, featuring more strenuous ascents and varied terrain compared to gentler riverside walks.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: The Río Tea and Alto do Marco Loop is a substantial hike, covering nearly 19 km. Hikers should plan for a duration of approximately 5 hours and 45 minutes to complete the entire loop, depending on pace and stops.

faqs.question: Where is the trailhead and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route, often starting near the fluvial beaches of the Río Tea. Specific parking details for the 'A Freixa a Salgueirón' route are not explicitly provided, but starting points near recreational areas or villages like Mondariz-Balneario typically offer parking options.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse landscapes, from lush riparian forests along the Río Tea, featuring alders, oaks, birches, and chestnuts, to areas with dense ferns and brambles. The terrain includes pleasant walks alongside the river, cultivated areas, and more demanding sections as you ascend towards Alto do Marco. Expect a mix of natural paths and historical elements.

faqs.question: Are there any specific points of interest or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can enjoy the Poza de Barco Beach – Tea River, admire the Salgueirón Rock Formation, and visit A Fábrica da Lus. The trail also passes ancient bridges like the Roman-origin Cernadela bridge and other medieval structures, as well as remnants of old watermills.

faqs.question: Is the Río Tea and Alto do Marco Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, specific regulations for dogs on this particular loop are not detailed. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas like the ZEC Río Tea, to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for the ZEC Río Tea area?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the ZEC Río Tea (Special Area of Conservation). Generally, there are no entrance fees for hiking in these areas. However, it's always good practice to respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and avoid disturbing the natural environment. No specific permits are usually required for recreational hiking.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: The Río Tea and Alto do Marco Loop can be enjoyed in various seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant foliage. During warmer months, the river beaches provide opportunities for relaxation. Always check local weather conditions before heading out, especially given the trail's difficult rating.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. You will hike along parts of the Río Tea, Sendero del río Tea, Sendeiro das Greas, and Camiño do río Tea. It also crosses the Puente de A Freixa.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or accommodation nearby?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through or near Mondariz-Balneario, a town known for its historic spa and amenities. You can find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options in this area, making it a convenient stop before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this difficult hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficult rating and nearly 19 km length, it's essential to be well-prepared. Bring plenty of water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear with good ankle support, weather-appropriate clothing, a map or GPS device (like the komoot app), and a first-aid kit. Trekking poles can also be beneficial for the varied terrain and ascents.

waypoints.name: Poza de Barco Beach – Tea River
